perturbation.original_text: (a) Each member of the ValueAct Group agrees that, during the Covered Period, (unless specifically requested in writing by the Company, acting through a resolution of a majority of the Company's directors not including the ValueAct Designee), it shall not, and shall cause each of its Affiliates or Associates (as such terms are defined in Rule 12b-2 promulgated by the SEC under the Exchange Act) (collectively and individually, the "ValueAct Affiliates," provided that no portfolio company of the ValueAct Group shall be deemed a "ValueAct Affiliate" so long as such portfolio company (A) has not discussed any of the actions set forth in this subsection (a) with the ValueAct Group or the ValueAct Designee, (B) has not received from the ValueAct Group or the ValueAct Designee information concerning the Company or its business, and (C) is not acting at the request of, in coordination with or on behalf of the ValueAct Group or the ValueAct Designee), not to, directly or indirectly, in any manner, alone or in concert with others:
perturbation.changed_text: (a) Each member of the ValueAct Group agrees that, during the Covered Period, (unless specifically requested orally by the Company's CEO), it shall not, and shall cause each of its Affiliates or Associates (as such terms are defined in Rule 12b-2 promulgated by the SEC under the Exchange Act) (collectively and individually, the "ValueAct Affiliates," provided that no portfolio company of the ValueAct Group shall be deemed a "ValueAct Affiliate" so long as such portfolio company (A) has not discussed any of the actions set forth in this subsection (a) with the ValueAct Group or the ValueAct Designee, (B) has not received from the ValueAct Group or the ValueAct Designee information concerning the Company or its business, and (C) is not acting at the request of, in coordination with or on behalf of the ValueAct Group or the ValueAct Designee), not to, directly or indirectly, in any manner, alone or in concert with others:
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'unless specifically requested in writing by the Company, acting through a resolution of a majority of the Company's directors not including the ValueAct Designee' to 'unless specifically requested orally by the Company's CEO' introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 11 requires amendments to be in writing. This change allows for oral requests, undermining the written amendment requirement in Section 11 and creating ambiguity about which provision controls.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be amended only by an agreement in writing executed by the parties hereto, and no waiver of compliance with any provision or condition of this Agreement and no consent provided for in this Agreement shall be effective unless evidenced by a written instrument executed by the party against whom such waiver or consent is to be effective. No failure or delay by a party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows for oral requests by the Company's CEO, which directly contradicts the contradicted text that explicitly states that any amendment to the agreement must be in writing. This creates a clear conflict and potential unenforceability issue, as it's unclear whether an oral request would be considered a valid amendment, given the requirement for written agreements.

perturbation.original_text: The ValueAct Designee will promptly advise the Nominating Committee if he ceases to satisfy any of the Conditions.
perturbation.changed_text: The ValueAct Designee will not be required to advise the Nominating Committee if he ceases to satisfy any of the Conditions.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the text from 'The ValueAct Designee will promptly advise the Nominating Committee if he ceases to satisfy any of the Conditions.' to 'The ValueAct Designee will not be required to advise the Nominating Committee if he ceases to satisfy any of the Conditions.' creates an in-text contradiction with Section 1(f). Section 1(f) imposes an obligation on the ValueAct Designee to resign if he ceases to satisfy the conditions set forth in clauses (c)-(d), which include meeting independence standards. By removing the requirement to advise the Nominating Committee, it becomes difficult for the Company to know if the Designee is still meeting those conditions, potentially triggering or nullifying the conditions set in Section 1(f).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) The Company's obligations hereunder shall terminate immediately, and the ValueAct Designee shall promptly offer to resign from the Board, and any committee of the Board on which he then sits (and, if requested by the Company, promptly deliver his written resignation to the Board (which shall provide for his immediate resignation) it being understood that it shall be in the Board's sole discretion whether to accept or reject such resignation) if: (i) members of the ValueAct Group, collectively, cease to beneficially own at least 7.5% of the Company's outstanding Common Stock; (ii) the ValueAct Designee ceases to satisfy the conditions set forth in clauses (c)-(d) above; (iii) a member of the ValueAct Group, including the ValueAct Designee, otherwise ceases to comply or breaches any of the terms of this Agreement or the Confidentiality Agreement; or (iv) the employment of the ValueAct Designee with the ValueAct Group is terminated for any reason. The ValueAct Group agrees to cause the ValueAct Designee to resign from the Board if the ValueAct Designee fails to resign if and when requested pursuant to this Section 1(f). Notwithstanding the foregoing, in the event of the occurrence of an event set forth in subsection (ii) or (iv) above, the provisions of Section 1(e) must be complied with before the Company's obligations hereunder may terminate.
perturbation.justification: The modified text directly contradicts Section 1(f). The original text required the ValueAct Designee to promptly advise the Nominating Committee if he ceased to satisfy any of the conditions. The changed text removes this requirement. Section 1(f) states that the ValueAct Designee shall promptly offer to resign from the Board if he ceases to satisfy the conditions set forth in clauses (c)-(d). By removing the requirement for the ValueAct Designee to advise the Nominating Committee, the Company may not be aware that the conditions in clauses (c)-(d) are no longer met, thereby contradicting the resignation requirements outlined in Section 1(f). This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as different sections of the contract now provide conflicting obligations, creating in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, the ValueAct Group currently beneficially owns 19,125,204 shares of the common stock, par value $0.01 per share, of the Company (the "Common Stock"), which represents approximately 10.77% of the issued and outstanding shares of Common Stock.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, the ValueAct Group currently beneficially owns 19,125,204 shares of the common stock, par value $0.01 per share, of the Company (the "Common Stock"), which represents approximately 15% of the issued and outstanding shares of Common Stock.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the percentage of ownership from 10.77% to 15% creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2(a)(iii), which states that the ValueAct Group cannot acquire securities that would result in them owning more than 12% of the Common Stock. This creates uncertainty because the "WHEREAS" clause is meant to be a statement of fact. There is a contradiction on their supposed existing holdings and the restrictions placed later on.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the percentage of ownership of the ValueAct Group from 10.77% to 15%. This directly contradicts Section 2(a)(iii), which explicitly states that the ValueAct Group cannot acquire securities that would result in them owning more than 12% of the Common Stock. The 'WHEREAS' clause represents a statement of fact about the existing ownership, and increasing it to 15% means that the ValueAct Group is in violation of the stated limit in Section 2(a)(iii), creating a clear contradiction.
